mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 6.5%, it signals a structural shift in labor market slack that historically precedes wage growth acceleration and consumer spending expansion, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or zero predictive power for 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances across a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and residential fixed investment indicates a broad-based demand recovery that lifts corporate revenue expectations across cyclical sectors, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in more than 60% of rolling 3-year windows, or the cross-sectional correlation between the composite index and forward returns drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production acts as a leading indicator for near-term corporate profit revisions during late-cycle expansions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as earnings momentum accelerates.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO rises for three consecutive months, invalidating the supply-side momentum premise.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-run trend rate of 2.5% signals sustained aggregate demand expansion, which historically supports higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if the rolling 3-year Spearman correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.10, or if forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during high-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When headline inflation accelerates past 3.0% annualized, it forces market participants to price in tighter monetary policy and higher real discount rates, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of triggered episodes, or forward returns remain positive despite sustained inflation readings above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent job creation acceleration and unemployment rate decline confirm a self-reinforcing labor market tightening that boosts household income expectations and near-term consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The composite labor signal yields a negative or neutral predictive rank correlation for 5-day returns in more than 40% of out-of-sample windows, refuting the near-term income channel.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 2.0% annualized alongside rising industrial production, it confirms synchronized demand and supply expansion that historically precedes earnings upgrades, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where both series confirm the threshold, or if the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10).
